concerns.text: SC-001 requires ingesting >1,000 entries, but T013a only mandates 'streaming' and T008a tests with 10 materials. No task explicitly verifies the pipeline handles the full >1,000 volume required by the spec.

SC-002 requires MAPE < 15% on unseen families. T017 splits by prototype, but no task explicitly validates that the resulting test set contains ZERO overlapping families with training, relying only on a runtime check in T021a which depends on `family_id` not guaranteed in `split_indices`.

SC-005 requires identifying 3 descriptors with p < 0.05. T027c3 mandates 'exit with code 1' if fewer are found, turning a success criterion (target) into a hard pipeline failure, which contradicts the spec's nature as a research target.

SC-003 requires < 100ms inference on CPU. T036 hard-codes a specific hardware baseline ('Intel Xeon E5-2680 v4'), creating a portability risk and contradicting the generic 'CPU-only' constraint in the spec/plan.

Plan 'Key Decisions' specifies a 'Unified loader' abstracting Materials Project/AFLOW. T009/T013a focus on single-source download/validation but lack a task explicitly implementing the abstraction layer logic to switch between sources dynamically.

SC-004 requires peak memory < 7GB. T008a tests with a 10-material sample. No task verifies memory usage scales correctly with the >1,000 entries required by SC-001, risking undetected OOM on full datasets.

T033 logs terminology changes to `state/...yaml` but does not explicitly mandate updating the `updated_at` timestamp required by Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) when the artifact is modified.
